enonce.texte: Match the following recycling and environmental terms with their correct definitions. This exercise will help you master the key vocabulary needed to discuss recycling initiatives.

questions.texte: Match each term (a-e) with its correct definition (i-v).

sousQuestions.texte: Recycle
sousQuestions.reponse: To process used materials so they can be used again.

sousQuestions.texte: Reduce
sousQuestions.reponse: To make something smaller in size, amount, or degree.

sousQuestions.texte: Reuse
sousQuestions.reponse: To use something again instead of throwing it away.

sousQuestions.texte: Waste
sousQuestions.reponse: Materials that are no longer needed and are thrown away.

sousQuestions.texte: Environment
sousQuestions.reponse: The natural world in which people, animals, and plants live.

questions.texte: Fill in the blank with the correct word from the list: [recycle, reduce, reuse, waste, environment].
questions.reponse: We should recycle plastic bottles to protect the environment.

sousQuestions.texte: We should _______ plastic bottles to protect the _______.
sousQuestions.reponse: recycle, environment

sousQuestions.texte: To _______ waste, we can _______ old jars instead of buying new containers.
sousQuestions.reponse: reduce, reuse

enonce.texte:
Read the following text about the 3 R's of environmental conservation and answer the questions below.

The 3 R's: Reduce, Reuse, Recycle

The 3 R's are the foundation of environmental conservation. Reduce means using fewer resources and creating less waste. For example, you can reduce waste by buying products with less packaging. Reuse means using items multiple times instead of throwing them away after one use. For instance, you can reuse glass jars as storage containers. Recycle means processing used materials to create new products. Recycling paper, plastic, and glass helps conserve natural resources and reduces pollution.

questions.texte: Answer the following questions based on the text.

sousQuestions.texte: What does 'Reduce' mean according to the text?
sousQuestions.reponse: Using fewer resources and creating less waste.

sousQuestions.texte: Give an example of 'Reuse' from the text.
sousQuestions.reponse: Reusing glass jars as storage containers.

sousQuestions.texte: Why is recycling important?
sousQuestions.reponse: Recycling helps conserve natural resources and reduces pollution.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain the difference between 'Reuse' and 'Recycle'.
sousQuestions.reponse: Reuse means using something again without processing it, while recycle means processing used materials to create new products.

enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences using the correct form of the verb in parentheses to create a gerund that functions as the subject of the sentence.

questions.texte: Complete each sentence with the gerund form of the verb in parentheses.

sousQuestions.texte: _______ (recycle) helps preserve the environment.
sousQuestions.reponse: Recycling

sousQuestions.texte: _______ (reduce) waste is essential for a sustainable future.
sousQuestions.reponse: Reducing

sousQuestions.texte: _______ (reuse) old items can save money and resources.
sousQuestions.reponse: Reusing

sousQuestions.texte: _______ (throw) away plastic bags harms the environment.
sousQuestions.reponse: Throwing

questions.texte: Rewrite the following sentences using a gerund as the subject.

sousQuestions.texte: It is important to recycle plastic.
sousQuestions.reponse: Recycling plastic is important.

sousQuestions.texte: It is beneficial to reuse glass bottles.
sousQuestions.reponse: Reusing glass bottles is beneficial.

enonce.texte: Listen to a radio report about a new recycling initiative in Douala. Take notes on the key information and answer the questions below.

questions.texte: Listen carefully to the report and answer the following questions.

sousQuestions.texte: What is the name of the recycling initiative mentioned?
sousQuestions.reponse: The Green Douala Project (example answer)

sousQuestions.texte: What materials are being collected for recycling?
sousQuestions.reponse: Plastic bottles, glass, and paper (example answer)

sousQuestions.texte: Where can people drop off their recyclable materials?
sousQuestions.reponse: At designated collection points in different neighborhoods (example answer)

sousQuestions.texte: According to the report, what are the benefits of this initiative?
sousQuestions.reponse: Reducing waste, creating jobs, and protecting the environment (example answer)

enonce.texte: Based on the radio report you just listened to about the recycling initiative, write a list of simple eco-friendly tips for the community. Use at least three gerunds as subjects in your sentences.

questions.texte: Write a list of 5-7 eco-friendly tips for your community.
questions.reponse: A well-structured list of eco-friendly tips using gerunds as subjects.

sousQuestions.texte: Example: Recycling plastic bottles helps reduce pollution.
sousQuestions.reponse: Recycling plastic bottles helps reduce pollution.

sousQuestions.texte: Example: Using reusable bags is better than using plastic ones.
sousQuestions.reponse: Using reusable bags is better than using plastic ones.

sousQuestions.texte: Example: Separating waste properly makes recycling easier.
sousQuestions.reponse: Separating waste properly makes recycling easier.

enonce.texte: Design a simple poster to promote recycling in your school or community. Your poster should include a clear message, simple drawings or symbols, and persuasive language encouraging people to recycle.
images: An example of a recycling campaign poster with the slogan 'Reduce, Reuse, Recycle' and symbols for plastic, glass, and paper recycling.

questions.texte: Create a recycling campaign poster with the following elements:

sousQuestions.texte: A catchy slogan or title
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'Recycle Today, Save Tomorrow'

sousQuestions.texte: At least 3 simple tips using gerunds as subjects
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'Recycling paper saves trees.', 'Reusing glass reduces waste.', 'Separating trash helps recycling.'

sousQuestions.texte: Visual elements (drawings or symbols)
sousQuestions.reponse: Recycling symbols, images of recyclable materials.

enonce.texte:
Read the following case study about a community recycling program and answer the questions.

Case Study: The Buea Recycling Initiative

In Buea, a community recycling program was launched in 2022. The program collects plastic bottles, paper, and glass from households. Residents are encouraged to separate their waste into different bins. The collected materials are then sent to a processing center where they are turned into new products. The program has created 15 jobs and has reduced landfill waste by 40% in its first year.

questions.texte: Analyze the case study and answer the following questions.

sousQuestions.texte: When was the recycling program launched?
What materials are collected in the program?
sousQuestions.reponse: Plastic bottles, paper, and glass.

sousQuestions.texte: Evaluate the success of this recycling program.
sousQuestions.reponse: The program has been successful. It created 15 jobs and reduced landfill waste by 40%.

sousQuestions.texte: Propose two improvements that could make the program even more effective.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 1. Expanding collection to more neighborhoods. 2. Organizing educational campaigns to encourage more participation.

intitule: Writing a Proposal for a School Recycling Program

enonce.texte: Write a proposal to the school administration suggesting the implementation of a recycling program at your school. Your proposal should be formal, well-structured, and persuasive.

questions.texte: Write a formal proposal that includes the following sections:

sousQuestions.texte: Title and introduction of the proposal
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'Proposal for the Implementation of a School Recycling Program'

sousQuestions.texte: The problem (current waste situation at school)
sousQuestions.reponse: Description of the current waste management issues at school.

sousQuestions.texte: The proposed solution (the recycling program)
sousQuestions.reponse: Detailed description of how the recycling program would work.

sousQuestions.texte: Benefits of the program
sousQuestions.reponse: List of environmental and educational benefits.

sousQuestions.texte: Conclusion and call to action
sousQuestions.reponse: A persuasive conclusion asking for approval.

enonce.texte: Research and compare recycling practices in your community with those in another country of your choice. Use a gerund as the subject in at least three sentences.

questions.texte: Write a comparative analysis of recycling practices.

sousQuestions.texte: Describe the recycling practices in your community.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'Recycling plastic bottles is common in some neighborhoods.'

sousQuestions.texte: Describe the recycling practices in the chosen country.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'Recycling glass is mandatory in Germany.'

sousQuestions.texte: Compare the two systems and identify strengths and weaknesses.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'Recycling in Germany is more efficient because of government regulations.'

sousQuestions.texte: Suggest improvements for your community based on the comparison.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'Adopting better separation systems would improve recycling rates.'
